title: Baissea kidengensis
taxonomy: Baissea kidengensis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. This family is a part of the order Gentianales, and it consists of over 5,000 species, including many species of vines and shrubs. Baissea kidengensis is a tropical plant native to the island of Madagascar. The classification of Baissea kidengensis is further divided into the genus Baissea, which contains about seven species. The classification of Baissea kidengensis is still being researched and refined by botanists and taxonomists.

intro: Baissea kidengensis is a climbing woody vine plant native to the tropical regions of West Africa. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in traditional medicine for treating various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Baissea kidengensis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
